data.title: China Next-Gen Appoints New CEO Amid Strategic Leadership Restructuring
data.description:
S&T Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:3928) ) just unveiled an announcement.

China Next-Gen Commerce and Supply Chain Limited has appointed Ms. Wang Kelly as the new Chief Executive Officer, effective from September 26, 2025. Ms. Wang, who is also the chairperson of the board, will lead the company in formulating a long-term business strategy and exploring new business opportunities. Despite the deviation from the Corporate Governance Code by combining the roles of chairperson and CEO, the board believes this will ensure consistent leadership and effective strategic planning. Additionally, Mr. Koh Chew Chiang has been redesignated as Co-Chief Executive Officer, maintaining a balance of power within the management team to protect the interests of the company and its shareholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:3928) stock is a Hold with a HK$4.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on S&T Holdings Ltd. stock, see the HK:3928 Stock Forecast page.

More about S&T Holdings Ltd.

The company focuses on enhancing its operations and business activities, aiming to develop a long-term business strategy and explore new opportunities to expand its geographical coverage and offerings.

data.description:

data.title: China Minsheng Banking Restructures Corporate Governance
data.description:
The latest update is out from China Minsheng Banking ( (HK:1988) ).

China Minsheng Banking Corp., Ltd. has received approval from the National Financial Regulatory Administration for amendments to its Articles of Association, which include the abolition of its Board of Supervisors. This change, effective immediately, shifts the supervisory responsibilities to the Audit Committee under the Board of Directors, aligning with regulatory requirements. The dissolution of the Board of Supervisors marks a significant shift in the bank’s corporate governance structure, potentially impacting its operational oversight and stakeholder engagement.

data.title: APAC Resources Reports Decreased Revenue but Maintains Profitability in 2025
data.description:
An announcement from APAC Resources ( (HK:1104) ) is now available.

APAC Resources Limited announced its audited consolidated final results for the year ended 30 June 2025, reporting a significant decline in revenue from HK$1,188,528,000 in 2024 to HK$353,131,000 in 2025. Despite the drop in revenue, the company achieved a profit of HK$240,774,000, down from HK$363,441,000 the previous year, with total comprehensive income for the year amounting to HK$261,729,000. The results reflect challenges in the trading sector, with a notable decrease in trading revenue, but the company managed to maintain profitability through other gains and income sources.
